headline: How The Exelon (EXC) Story Is Shifting On Regulation Capex And Valuation Expectations
summary: Exelon's updated fair value estimate has shifted modestly, moving from about US$51.41 to roughly US$50.17 per share, giving you a slightly different reference point for what analysts think the stock may be worth. This adjustment sits alongside a mixed set of Street views, with some firms trimming targets while others lift them on the back of refreshed models, new EPS guidance, and a larger capex plan. As you read on, you will see how these price target moves fit into the broader, evolving...

headline: How Efficiency Milestones and Leadership Shifts Will Impact Exelon (EXC) Investors
summary: Exelon’s utilities reported that ComEd’s Energy Efficiency Program has helped northern Illinois customers save about US$13.00 billion on energy bills since 2008, while PECO announced a leadership change with COO Mike Innocenzo stepping in as interim President and CEO and David Vahos moving to a Special Advisor role. Together, these developments highlight Exelon’s emphasis on customer cost relief and disciplined operational management across its major regulated utility subsidiaries. We’ll now...
